title: US — your first 90 days
banner: Educational, not financial advice. Employer plans differ — read your own documents.

items.title: Apply for your SSN
items.why: Everything downstream — payroll, credit, bank bonuses — keys off it. Apply as soon as your status allows.

items.title: Open a checking account
items.why: You need a US account for payroll on day one; large banks open accounts with passport + I-94 before the SSN arrives.

items.title: Start credit history with a secured/starter card
items.why: Your credit age starts the day your first card opens. Two years of clean history is what unlocks good rates later.

items.title: Sanity-check your W-4 withholding
items.why: Wrong withholding = an interest-free loan to the IRS, or a surprise bill in April.

items.title: Make your employer insurance elections in the enrollment window
items.why: Miss the window and you wait for open enrollment. Health, dental, vision — and check if an HDHP+HSA fits you.

items.title: Enroll in 401(k) at least to the full employer match
items.why: The match is a 50–100% instant return. Nothing else you do this year beats it.

items.title: Set beneficiary designations everywhere
items.why: 401(k), insurance, bank accounts. Beneficiaries override wills — five minutes now saves your family a legal mess.

items.title: Get renter's insurance
items.why: ~$15/month covers everything you own; most landlords require it anyway.

items.title: Set your emergency-fund target band
items.why: On a visa, your emergency fund is also your immigration runway — 6 months beats the standard 3 for H-1B holders (job loss starts a 60-day clock).

items.title: Get your state ID / driver's license
items.why: Carrying your passport everywhere is a bad idea; most states want you licensed within 30–90 days of residency.

items.title: Save your I-94 after EVERY entry
items.why: The I-94, not the visa stamp, controls your authorized stay. A wrong I-94 caught early is a fix; caught late it's a status problem.
